case.focal_claim: The average plumbing firm whose name begins with A or a number receives more service complaints than other firms.

execution_overview.overall_summary: Executed the planned negative binomial analyses in Python. Found a positive, statistically significant association between A/number-leading firm names (first_A) and total 2008 complaints. The effect persists with controls. Poisson vs NB LLR strongly favors the NB model. Chi-square and Welch t-test are also significant in the expected direction.

deviations.description: Rewrote Poisson and Negative Binomial estimation to MLE via SciPy to remove the statsmodels dependency and ensured results are saved to /app/artifacts/execution_result.json.
deviations.reason: Execution failed due to ModuleNotFoundError: statsmodels in the container.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned path (chi-square, Poisson, NB, model comparison, NB with controls, t-test). Zero-inflated models were optional and skipped.

test_statistics.other: LLR Poisson vs NB: LLR=3100.621, p<1e-6; Chi-square (binary complaints by first_A): chi2=62.597, p<1e-6; Poisson GOF p≈1.12e-191 (overdispersion).

result_raw.result_text: In the NB model without controls, first_A is positively associated with complaints (coef≈1.685, z≈5.51, p<1e-6), indicating A/number-leading firms receive more complaints.

conclusion.conclusion_text: The result supports the focal claim: firms with names beginning with A or a number receive more complaints.

deviations.description: Implemented NB (and Poisson) MLE using SciPy to avoid the missing statsmodels package; saved standardized outputs.
deviations.reason: Original Python translation relied on statsmodels which was not available at runtime.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Ran NB with controls on overall complaints including chicago as a control as instructed.

test_statistics.other: Controls included: multiple_names, on_google, ad_spend_k, firm_age, chicago, emp_size; NB alpha≈7.612.

result_raw.result_text: In the NB model with controls, first_A remains positively associated with complaints (coef≈0.676, z≈3.21, p≈0.0013).

conclusion.conclusion_text: The controlled NB model indicates that A/number-leading firms receive more complaints, supporting the focal claim.
